mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.2% in May 2012 reflects post-crisis labor slack
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with declining unemployment trend
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant positive correlation with month-over-month UNRATE declines (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 0.25% signals an extended zero-lower-bound monetary regime that maintains low discount rates and encourages equity risk-taking, sustaining positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% in May 2012 reflects post-2008 crisis accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate exceeds 3-month moving average by 0.5% or more
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 404,596M in May 2012 reflects post-recession consumer spending recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns following RSAFS acceleration are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.29 in May 2012 reflects post-crisis industrial recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or month-over-month INDPRO growth shows no significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with stable inflation signals a sustainable labor market expansion without wage-price spiral risk, supporting equity valuations through improved earnings quality and lower inflation risk premium, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133,009k and CPIAUCSL at 228.53 in May 2012 reflect moderate labor gains with contained inflation
regime_hint: goldilocks expansion with moderate employment growth and low inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (PAYEMS + CPIAUCSL interaction) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline combined with rising unemployment signals demand weakness and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 717k and UNRATE at 8.2% in May 2012 reflect post-crisis housing weakness
regime_hint: demand contraction with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets react to tightening signals.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 228.53 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% in May 2012 reflect low inflation and accommodative policy
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth deceleration signals weakening economic momentum and falling corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity investors reprice growth and discount rates adjust downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate falls below 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,491B (2017 dollars) in Q1 2012 reflects post-crisis recovery deceleration
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with slowing growth momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of periods with GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
